# @joakimbugge/mikroorm-seeder

> Decorator-based entity seeding for MikroORM. Annotate entity properties with `@Seed()` to describe how they should be populated, then create or persist fully populated entity graphs — including relations, embedded types, and circular guards — with a single call.

Full documentation: https://joakimbugge.github.io/seeders/mikroorm/

## Installation

```bash
npm install @joakimbugge/mikroorm-seeder @mikro-orm/core
```

Works with both `ReflectMetadataProvider` and `TsMorphMetadataProvider`.

**ES decorators are not supported.** Requires legacy decorators (`experimentalDecorators: true`). The newer TC39 ES decorator proposal (used via `@mikro-orm/decorators`) is not compatible — relations and embedded properties will not be auto-seeded with it.

**`defineEntity()` is not supported.** Use standard MikroORM class decorators (`@Entity()`, `@Property()`, etc.).

## @Seed() decorator

Import entity decorators from `@mikro-orm/decorators/legacy`. Annotate scalar properties with a factory function; relation properties take the bare decorator or a `count` option.

```ts
import { Entity, PrimaryKey, Property, OneToMany, ManyToOne } from '@mikro-orm/decorators/legacy'
import { faker } from '@faker-js/faker'
import { Seed } from '@joakimbugge/mikroorm-seeder'

@Entity()
class Author {
  @PrimaryKey()
  id!: number

  @Seed(() => faker.person.fullName())
  @Property()
  name!: string

  @Seed({ count: 3 })           // seed 3 related Book instances
  @OneToMany(() => Book, (b) => b.author)
  books!: Book[]
}

@Entity()
class Book {
  @Seed(() => faker.lorem.words(4))
  @Property()
  title!: string

  @Seed()                        // bare decorator on a to-one relation
  @ManyToOne(() => Author)
  author!: Author
}
```

### Sequence index

The factory receives a zero-based index as its third argument — counts up across `createMany`/`saveMany` batches:

```ts
@Seed((_, __, i) => `user-${i}@example.com`)
@Property()
email!: string
```

### Depending on earlier properties

Properties are seeded in declaration order. Each factory receives the partially-built entity as its second argument:

```ts
@Seed(() => faker.date.past())
@Property()
beginDate!: Date

@Seed((_, self: Event) => faker.date.future({ refDate: self.beginDate }))
@Property()
endDate!: Date
```

### Circular guard

When seeding `Author`, each `Book`'s `author` back-relation is left `undefined` to prevent infinite recursion. Seeding a `Book` directly works fine and does populate `book.author`.

### Embedded types

`@Embedded` properties are seeded automatically — annotate the embedded class's properties with `@Seed()`:

```ts
@Embeddable()
class Address {
  @Seed(() => faker.location.streetAddress())
  @Property()
  street!: string

  @Seed(() => faker.location.city())
  @Property()
  city!: string
}

@Entity()
class User {
  @Seed()
  @Embedded(() => Address)
  address!: Address
}

const user = await create(User)
// user.address → fully populated Address instance
```

## seed() builder

`seed(EntityClass)` returns a builder with four methods.

### save / saveMany — persist to database

Pass an `EntityManager` in the options:

```ts
import { seed } from '@joakimbugge/mikroorm-seeder'

const author = await seed(Author).save({ em })
// author.id    → assigned by the database
// author.books → 3 persisted Book instances

const authors = await seed(Author).saveMany(5, { em })
```

### create / createMany — build without persisting

```ts
const author = await seed(Author).create()
const books = await seed(Book).createMany(10)
```

### Multiple entity types at once

```ts
const [author, book] = await seed([Author, Book]).create()
// Relations between Author and Book are disabled by default

const [author, book] = await seed([Author, Book]).save({ em, relations: true })
// relations: true enables cross-entity relation seeding
```

`createMany`/`saveMany` return an array per class:

```ts
const [authors, books] = await seed([Author, Book]).createMany(3)
```

### relations option

```ts
const author = await seed(Author).create({ relations: false })
// author.books → undefined
```

### values option — override seeded properties

```ts
const booking = await seed(Booking).save({ em, values: { status: 'confirmed' } })

// Per-instance factory in values:
const bookings = await seed(Booking).saveMany(10, {
  em,
  values: {
    price: () => faker.number.float({ min: 10, max: 500 }),
    status: 'confirmed',
  },
})
```

`values` runs after all `@Seed` factories and wins unconditionally. Works on properties with no `@Seed` decorator too.

### Accessing EntityManager inside a factory

```ts
@Seed(async ({ em }) => {
  return em!.findOneOrFail(Role, { name: 'admin' })
})
@ManyToOne(() => Role)
role!: Role
```

## @Seeder and runSeeders

```ts
import { Seeder, runSeeders, seed } from '@joakimbugge/mikroorm-seeder'
import type { SeederInterface, SeedContext } from '@joakimbugge/mikroorm-seeder'

@Seeder()
class UserSeeder implements SeederInterface {
  async run(ctx: SeedContext) {
    await seed(User).saveMany(10, ctx)
  }
}

@Seeder({ dependencies: [UserSeeder] })
class PostSeeder implements SeederInterface {
  async run(ctx: SeedContext) {
    await seed(Post).saveMany(50, ctx)
  }
}

await runSeeders([PostSeeder], { em })
// UserSeeder runs first, then PostSeeder
```

- Dependencies always complete before the seeders that depend on them.
- Seeders with no dependency relationship run **concurrently**.
- A seeder is run at most once, even if declared multiple times.
- Circular dependencies are detected at runtime and throw.

### Returning seeded entities

```ts
const results = await runSeeders([UserSeeder], { em })
const users = results.get(UserSeeder) as User[]
```

### Using with MikroORM's native seeder package

`@Seed()` and `seed()` work inside MikroORM's own `@mikro-orm/seeder` Seeder classes:

```ts
import { Seeder } from '@mikro-orm/seeder'
import type { EntityManager } from '@mikro-orm/core'
import { seed } from '@joakimbugge/mikroorm-seeder'

export class UserSeeder extends Seeder {
  async run(em: EntityManager): Promise<void> {
    await seed(User).saveMany(10, { em })
  }
}
```

## CLI

Install `ts-node` as a dev dependency to use `.ts` files directly. Pass `--orm` (`-o`) with a path to a file that exports a `MikroORM` instance:

```ts
// orm.ts
import { MikroORM } from '@mikro-orm/core'
export default await MikroORM.init({ ... })
```

```bash
# Run all @Seeder classes from a glob pattern
npx @joakimbugge/mikroorm-seeder seed:run './src/seeders/*.ts' -o ./src/orm.ts

# Persist N instances of every entity that has @Seed decorators
npx @joakimbugge/mikroorm-seeder seed:entities './src/entities/*.ts' -o ./src/orm.ts --count 20

# List seeder run history
npx @joakimbugge/mikroorm-seeder seed:list -o ./src/orm.ts

# Remove a seeder from history so it runs again
npx @joakimbugge/mikroorm-seeder seed:untrack UserSeeder -o ./src/orm.ts
```

Pass `--dry-run` (`-n`) to `seed:run` or `seed:entities` to preview without writing to the database.

If `-o` is omitted, the CLI looks for `mikroorm-seeder.config.ts` then `mikroorm-seeder.config.js` in the current directory.

npm scripts shorthand:

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "seed:run": "mikroorm-seeder seed:run './src/seeders/*.ts' -o ./src/orm.ts"
  }
}
```

## Logging

```ts
// Console logger
await runSeeders([UserSeeder], { em, logging: true })

// Custom logger
await runSeeders([UserSeeder], {
  em,
  logging: true,
  logger: { log: (msg) => myLogger.info(msg), warn: (msg) => myLogger.warn(msg) },
})
```

## Hooks

```ts
await runSeeders([UserSeeder, PostSeeder], {
  em,
  onBefore: (seeder) => console.log(`Starting ${seeder.name}...`),
  onAfter: (seeder, durationMs) => console.log(`${seeder.name} done in ${durationMs}ms`),
  onError: (seeder, error) => console.error(`${seeder.name} failed`, error),
  skip: (seeder) => alreadyRun.has(seeder.name),  // return true to skip
})
```

## NestJS integration

Use `@joakimbugge/nest-mikroorm-seeder` for NestJS projects. It wraps `runSeeders` in a `SeederModule` that runs seeders on application bootstrap and tracks which have already run.

```ts
import { SeederModule } from '@joakimbugge/nest-mikroorm-seeder'

@Module({
  imports: [
    MikroOrmModule.forRoot({ ... }),
    SeederModule.forRoot({ seeders: [UserSeeder] }),
  ],
})
export class AppModule {}
```
